_note: Round-14 follow-up #6: an inline typed dispatcher whose member key is computed (`[EVENT]`) where `EVENT` is a top-level `const EVENT = 'hi'` should resolve to `'hi'` at synth time. Combined with a second typed dispatcher declaring `hi` as a static key, the duplicate-collapse path (round-8 #5) routes the shared name through the untyped layer so the typed mapped's `Omit<typed, keyof untyped> & untyped` overrides with `CustomEvent<any>`. Upstream's `ComponentEvents.ts:319` (`getName` → `getIdentifierValue` → `getVariableAtTopLevel`) walks top-level `const` decls and substitutes the string-literal initializer when the property key is a `ComputedPropertyName` over a bare identifier. Pre-fix native's `expression_collect_inline_typed_members` only accepted `StaticIdentifier` and `StringLiteral` keys, so the computed name was silently dropped from the duplicate-detection pass and the two typed sources intersected at `boolean & string` = `never` — every consumer handler was rejected. Post-fix `collect_inline_typed_dispatcher_member_names` pre-scans `const NAME = 'literal'` bindings via `collect_top_level_string_const_literals` and resolves each computed-identifier key against that map. Inner here declares two `<{ hi: ... }>` dispatchers (one via computed key, one static); Consumer's `on:hi={handleAny}` passes — pre-fix this fired TS2345 because `hi` collapsed to `CustomEvent<never>`.
